statement.en: Standardised Data Models for Advanced Logistics Solutions TIC4.0 - PEMA Collaboration Concept Paper
statement.summary: In May 2018, fifteen leading global container terminal operators and several port equipment manufacturers convened at Federation of European Private Port Companies and Terminals (FEPORT) headquarters in Brussels to address the pressing challenges facing the cargo handling sector. This gathering was driven by a shared recognition: the container terminal industry needed to proactively prepare for the Fourth Industrial Revolution and accelerate its digital transformation. The discussions revealed that evolving logistics chain dynamics required stronger technical collaboration between terminals and equipment manufacturers. By working together, these stakeholders could develop industry standards that would enable the adoption of Internet of Things technologies, Artificial Intelligence, and othe

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Document is a 2026-dated concept paper describing a 2018 convening and subsequent standardization proposal. Status is provisionally developmental—describes collaborative framework design rather than completed standard or empirically validated system. No refutation evidence present, but claims remain contingent on implementation and industry adoption.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 多くの港の融合は、摩擦を四乗根で減らすことで、共通の規格空間へ流れ込む。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 左右の力が中核で融合し、斜めの流れが四次産業革命の対角線を示唆する。
